codeReview.summary: The shaping-library worker added 3 well-written ligature tests (VAL-SHAPE-002) to complete coverage that the prior build-system-cargo-deps worker had left incomplete. The tests correctly use Variable Nunito Sans (which has GSUB ligature tables) and verify fi/fl ligature substitution with +liga and non-substitution with -liga. The full shaping library API (shape function, ShapedGlyph struct, Feature re-export) was already implemented by the build-system-cargo-deps worker and is correct: clean HarfRust wrapper, proper error handling for empty text and unparseable fonts, #[repr(C)] struct layout, values in font units as documented. All 5 required test categories from the feature description are covered across the 14 total tests. Two non-blocking issues noted.

issues.description: glyph_id truncation: `info.glyph_id as u16` silently truncates from u32. OpenType fonts support up to 65,535 glyphs so u16 is sufficient in practice, but the silent cast could mask bugs. A debug_assert!(info.glyph_id <= u16::MAX as u32) or u16::try_from() would be safer defense-in-depth.

read-fonts dependency is declared in the shaping library's Cargo.toml but is only used by the rasterize.rs submodule (added by a later feature). For the shaping API itself, only harfrust is needed. This is harmless since rasterize.rs lives in this crate, but it means the shaping library couples shaping and rasterization in one crate — a design choice rather than a bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The font-rendering.md library doc shows Feature::from_str("+liga").unwrap() as the API, but the actual harfrust 0.5 API uses the FromStr trait so the idiomatic call is "+liga".parse::<Feature>().unwrap(). Both build-system-cargo-deps and shaping-library workers used .parse() in tests. The build-system-cargo-deps worker flagged this in skillFeedback but the doc was not updated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/test/tests/shaping.rs line 68: `"+liga".parse::<Feature>().unwrap()` vs .factory/library/font-rendering.md showing `Feature::from_str("+liga").unwrap()`. build-system-cargo-deps handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0] also flags this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The font-rendering.md library doc shows `info.as_glyph()` for accessing glyph IDs, but that method is pub(crate) in harfrust. The actual API uses `info.glyph_id` (public u32 field). Workers had to discover this themselves.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/font-rendering.md 'Reading Output' section: `let glyph_id = info.as_glyph();` vs actual code in system/libraries/shaping/src/lib.rs:63: `glyph_id: info.glyph_id as u16`

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shaping-library feature overlapped heavily with build-system-cargo-deps: the build-system worker implemented the full shaping API and 11 of 14 tests. The shaping-library worker only needed to add 3 ligature tests. This suggests the feature boundaries were not well-defined — the build-system feature scope crept into shaping-library territory. The shaping-library worker noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: build-system-cargo-deps handoff: 'Created libraries/shaping/ with HarfRust integration... 11 new tests'. shaping-library handoff: 'The shaping library API... was already fully implemented by the previous build-system-cargo-deps worker.'

summary: PASS. The shaping library implementation is correct and complete. The core API (shape function returning Vec<ShapedGlyph> in font units) correctly wraps HarfRust with proper error handling. All 5 required test categories are covered: (1) basic Latin glyph count + non-zero advances, (2) empty string → 0 glyphs, (3) fi/fl ligatures with named font (Variable Nunito Sans with GSUB), (4) AV kerning tighter than sum, (5) +liga vs -liga feature control. 14 total tests, all passing. Two non-blocking issues: silent u32→u16 glyph_id truncation and shaping+rasterization coupling in one crate. Three shared state observations about font-rendering.md doc inaccuracies and feature boundary overlap.
